skill.name: flux-2-klein
skill.description: Generate images with Flux 2 Klein (Black Forest Labs' distilled fast variant of Flux 2) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model.
skill.summary: Generate professional images with Flux 2 Klein AI model on RunComfy with optimized prompting strategies
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: All 83 static findings are false positives. The flagged patterns (external_commands, filesystem, network) are markdown documentation and legitimate RunComfy service endpoints, not malicious code. The skill is a documentation file for an AI image generation CLI tool with no actual executable code. No command injection, data exfiltration, or malicious behavior detected.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 83 potential issues including 'external_commands', 'filesystem', and 'network' patterns. All are false positives: backticks are markdown code formatting, URLs are legitimate RunComfy service endpoints, and path references are documentation. No executable code or malicious behavior present.

content.user_title: Generate Images with Flux 2 Klein AI on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Create professional images rapidly with Flux 2 Klein AI. The skill provides optimized prompting strategies and model selection guidance to get better results than basic prompting.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using Flux 2 Klein 4B variant (sub-second latency for rapid iteration)
Generate images using Flux 2 Klein 9B variant (high-fidelity polish and detail)
Apply multi-reference style transfer with up to 4 reference images
Optimize prompts using subject-first declarative grammar for better output
Configure step counts for concepting (4-8 steps) vs polish (~25 steps) workflows
Handle brand-consistent asset generation with reference image alignment
limitations: Maximum resolution ceiling of ~2K; does not support 4K output
Aspect ratio capped at 16:9; extreme wide or tall ratios get cropped
Prompt length limited to ~512 tokens; longer prompts degrade quality
Text rendering less precise than specialized models like GPT Image 2

use_cases.title: Rapid Product Visualization
use_cases.description: Generate multiple product mockups quickly for e-commerce or marketing materials. Use 4B variant for fast iteration, then 9B for final polished output.

use_cases.title: Live Art Direction Sessions
use_cases.description: Collaborate in real-time on visual concepts with sub-second feedback. Refine compositions, styles, and compositions iteratively until the vision is achieved.

use_cases.title: Brand-Consistent Asset Generation
use_cases.description: Create unified visual assets using multi-reference styling. Maintain brand consistency across product images, banners, and marketing materials.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a [product type] on a [background/surface], with [lighting style], [camera settings], [style descriptor], [quality specification]
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - For users new to Flux 2 Klein prompting

prompt_templates.title: Brand-Style Product Shot
prompt_templates.prompt: Same composition and lighting as [reference image], but the [element] is now [new description]; keep [stable elements] exactly as in the reference

prompt_templates.title: Lifestyle Scene with Subject
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Subject-first declarative grammar for maximum adherence

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Brand Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate [subject description] matching the [style/aesthetic] of [reference images]. Apply same [lighting mood], [color palette], [composition style] across all outputs.

output_examples.input: A minimalist ceramic coffee mug on a natural wood surface, soft window light from the left, shallow depth of field, 50mm prime, f/2.0, neutral gray background, e-commerce ready
output_examples.output: A clean product photograph showing a ceramic mug centered on a wooden surface with soft natural lighting creating gentle shadows. The background is smoothly blurred, and the image is professionally lit for commercial use.

output_examples.input: A hummingbird mid-flight, iridescent emerald feathers, morning sun lighting, macro photography, cinematic
output_examples.output: A detailed close-up photograph of a hummingbird frozen in flight with vibrant, shimmering feathers catching golden morning light. The background features a softly blurred tropical garden with sharp focus on the bird.

output_examples.input: Modern athletic shoe, white background, product photography, studio lighting, 4K detail
output_examples.output: A crisp product shot of an athletic shoe on a pure white backdrop, professionally lit to highlight the shoe's texture and contours without harsh shadows.

best_practices: Use subject-first declarative grammar: state the subject first, then scene, style, lighting, and quality modifiers in order
Match step count to workflow phase: 4-8 steps for rapid concepting, ~25 steps for final polish on the 9B variant
Keep reference images in the same visual register (all photorealistic or all stylized) to avoid confusing the model
anti_patterns: Do not use conflicting adjectives like 'minimalist + ornate' as they cancel each other out
Do not exceed ~512 tokens in prompts; the model degrades rather than truncating gracefully
Do not request 4K resolution or ultra-wide aspect ratios; the model caps at ~2K and 16:9 respectively

faq.question: What is the difference between Flux 2 Klein 4B and 9B variants?
faq.answer: The 4B variant offers sub-second latency for rapid iteration and live art-direction. The 9B variant provides higher fidelity and better detail for polished final output. Use 4B for concepting, 9B for the final pass.

faq.question: How do I optimize prompts for Flux 2 Klein?
faq.answer: Use subject-first declarative grammar: Subject + action + scene + style + lighting + camera + quality. Front-load the subject and trail with technical directives. Specific technical terms (like '4K product photo') outperform vague descriptions.

faq.question: What step count should I use?
faq.answer: Use 4-8 steps on 4B for fast concepting. For refinement, 8-15 steps still on 4B. For final polish with maximum detail, use ~25 steps on 9B. Steps beyond 25 provide diminishing returns.

faq.question: Can I use reference images with Flux 2 Klein?
faq.answer: Yes, up to 4 simultaneous reference images are supported for style transfer and guided composition. Keep all reference images in the same visual register (all photographic or all stylized) for best results.

faq.question: Why is my text rendering poor in generated images?
faq.answer: Flux 2 Klein has an 8B Qwen3 embedder that provides decent text rendering, but it is not as precise as specialized models like GPT Image 2. For heavy embedded text or multilingual signage, route to GPT Image 2 instead.

faq.question: What are the resolution and aspect ratio limits?
faq.answer: Maximum resolution is approximately 2K total (not 4K). Aspect ratio is capped at 16:9. Requests for 4K or ultra-wide formats will be degraded or cropped.
